title: RWisecondorX Agent Guidelines
text: Build R toolkit copy number analysis trisomy prediction non-invasive prenatal testing (NIPT), comparable scope NIPTUtils built entirely Rduckhts/DuckDB rather Rsamtools/Python. package ports two upstream implementations: WisecondorX — copy number variation detection cfDNA (Python/pysam upstream) NIPTeR — fast trisomy prediction via chromosomal fraction Z-scores, NCV scores, regression, chi-squared correction (R/Rsamtools upstream) Design priorities: Exact conformance upstream implementations documented testable. Performance — DuckDB SQL replaces per-read Python loops Rsamtools scans; large BAMs run seconds, minutes. Interoperable file formats — bgzipped, tabix-indexed BED files intermediate layer binning analysis, consumable DuckDB, R, Python, tabix-aware tool. Python runtime dependency — Rduckhts replaces pysam HTS operations; reticulate condathis optional used conformance testing.

title: RWisecondorX Agent Guidelines
text: Shared binning engine — R/convert.R - bam_convert(): DuckDB/SQL read-counting core. Supports binsize, mapq, require_flags (samtools -f), exclude_flags (samtools -F), rmdup ("streaming" / "flag" / "none"), separate_strands (returns list forward/reverse data frames), CRAM via reference. Achieves exact bin--bin conformance WisecondorX HG00106.chrom11 (25,115 non-zero bins, 0 mismatches). - bam_convert_bed(): bgzipped + tabix-indexed 4-column BED output (chrom, start, end, count). Uses Rduckhts::rduckhts_bgzip() rduckhts_tabix_index() — external tools. WisecondorX layer - R/wisecondorx_cli.R: wisecondorx_convert(), wisecondorx_newref(), wisecondorx_predict() — thin condathis wrappers delegating official bioconda package. - R/wisecondorx_npz.R: bam_convert_npz() — WisecondorX-compatible NPZ output via reticulate. NIPTeR binning layer — R/nipter_bin.R - nipter_bin_bam(): produces NIPTeRSample objects. separate_strands = FALSE (default), class c("NIPTeRSample", "CombinedStrands"); separate_strands = TRUE, class c("NIPTeRSample", "SeparatedStrands") autosomal_chromosome_reads list two matrices (forward/reverse, rownames "1F".."22F" / "1R".."22R"). Exposes mapq, require_flags, exclude_flags, rmdup pre-filtering matching real-world NIPT pipelines (e.g. mapq=40L, exclude_flags=1024L). - nipter_bin_bam_bed(): BED output separate_strands support. FALSE (default): 5-column BED (chrom, start, end, count, corrected_count). TRUE: 9-column BED (chrom, start, end, count, count_fwd, count_rev, corrected_count, corrected_fwd, corrected_rev). Tests - inst/tinytest/test_fixtures.R (41 assertions): synthetic BAM/CRAM fixtures, three rmdup modes, CRAM reference round-trip. - inst/tinytest/test_nipter.R (47 assertions): NIPTeRSample structure, matrix dimensions rownames, MAPQ filter, exclude_flags=1024 dedup, BED.gz output shape, bin--bin conformance NIPTeR::bin_bam_sample() using bundled whole-genome fixture default (NIPTER_CONFORMANCE_BAM remains override). - inst/tinytest/test_cli_args.R: CLI argument builder unit tests. - inst/tinytest/test_npz.R: NPZ round-trip tests. - inst/tinytest/test_integration.R: WisecondorX bin--bin conformance via condathis. - inst/tinytest/test_sra_metadata.R: SRA URL helpers. Infrastructure - R/aaa.R: SRA metadata helpers. - scripts/make_fixtures.sh: reproducible synthetic BAM/CRAM fixture generation. - DESCRIPTION: NIPTeR authors (Dirk de Weerd, Lennart Johansson) listed cph. NIPTeR added Suggests. NIPTeR statistical layer file strictly separate; never mix NIPTeR WisecondorX code. statistical functions support CombinedStrands SeparatedStrands samples. R/nipter_control.R — nipter_as_control_group(): constructs NIPTeRControlGroup list NIPTeRSample objects validation dedup. nipter_diagnose_control_group(): per-chromosome Z-scores Shapiro-Wilk normality tests across control group. nipter_match_control_group(): selects best-fit control samples test sample; distance computation Rcpp+OpenMP nipter_ssd_scores_cpp() kernel (one query vs N columns pre-extracted 22×N fractions matrix). nipter_match_matrix(): computes full N×N pairwise SSD matrix via nipter_ssd_matrix_cpp() — replaces production lapply(1:N, match_control_group(..., mode="report")) hot loop single vectorized call. nipter_control_group_from_beds(): loads directory TSV.bgz files control group one DuckDB pass via rduckhts_tabix_multi(). Internal helpers .sample_chr_fractions() (44-element SeparatedStrands, 22 Combined) .sample_chr_fractions_collapsed() (always 22-element) handle strand dispatch. R/nipter_gc.R — nipter_gc_precompute(): runs rduckhts_fasta_nuc() writes 5-column TSV.bgz+tbi (chrom, start, end, pct_gc, seq_len); use pay FASTA scan cost per reference build rather per sample. nipter_gc_correct(): LOESS bin-weight GC correction accepting gc_table parameter — either path precomputed TSV.bgz, -memory list, NULL (falls back fasta path). correcting NIPTeRControlGroup, GC table resolved reused every sample. SeparatedStrands: LOESS fitted Reduce("+", auto_list) summed counts, corrections applied independently strand matrix via lapply(). Sex chromosome correction via nearest-neighbour (LOESS) -bucket weights (bin-weight). R/nipter_chi.R — nipter_chi_correct(): chi-squared overdispersion correction applied simultaneously sample control group. SeparatedStrands: chi computed summed strand counts, correction applied per-strand via lapply(). R/nipter_score.R — nipter_z_score(): chromosomal fraction Z-score Shapiro-Wilk normality test. Uses collapsed fractions SeparatedStrands. nipter_ncv_score(): normalised chromosome value brute-force denominator search using utils::combn() (replaces sets::set_combn() dependency). R/nipter_regression.R — nipter_regression(): forward stepwise regression Z-score train/test split, practical vs theoretical CV selection. Supports CombinedStrands SeparatedStrands. SeparatedStrands doubles predictor pool (44 candidates: "1F".."22F","1R".."22R") complementary exclusion (selecting "5F" excludes "5F" "5R" model). R/nipter_sex.R — nipter_sex_model(): 2-component GMM sex prediction via mclust::Mclust(), supporting "y_fraction" "xy_fraction" methods. nipter_sex_model_y_unique(): 2-component GMM Y-unique region read ratios (BAM-level, uses nipter_y_unique_ratio()). nipter_y_unique_ratio(): counts reads overlapping 7 Y-chromosome unique gene regions via DuckDB/duckhts index-based region queries returns ratio total nuclear reads. nipter_predict_sex(): classifies sample male/female using one models majority-vote consensus; y_unique_ratio parameter enables Y-unique model vote. Internal .mclust_fit() wrapper evaluates mclust namespace work around mclustBIC() namespace-qualified upstream mclust. inst/tinytest/test_nipter_stats.R — 105 assertions covering statistical functions including SeparatedStrands variants. inst/tinytest/test_nipter_sex.R — 45 assertions covering sex model building, Y-unique model, 3-model consensus, classification accuracy, prediction, edge cases. Native WisecondorX implementation (newref + predict) Pure R/Rcpp port WisecondorX newref predict pipelines, performance-critical KNN reference-finding compiled C++. Python runtime dependency. R/rwisecondorx_utils.R — Shared utilities: .train_gender_model() (2-component GMM Y-fractions zero-variance fallback), .gender_correct(), .get_mask() (5% median coverage threshold), .normalize_and_mask(), .train_pca() (5 components, ratio correction), .project_pc(), .predict_gender(). Also exports scale_sample() rescaling bin sizes. R/rwisecondorx_newref.R — rwisecondorx_newref(): reference building pipeline. Gender model training → global bin mask → per-partition (/F/M) normalize/PCA/distance-filter/KNN/null-ratios. .build_sub_reference() orchestrates partition. .get_reference() delegates Rcpp. R/rwisecondorx_predict.R — rwisecondorx_predict(): prediction pipeline. Rescale → predict gender → normalize autosomes (coverage + PCA + weights + optimal cutoff + 3-pass within-sample normalization aberration masking) → normalize gonosomes → combine → inflate → log-transform → blacklist → CBS → segment Z-scores → aberration calling. .normalize(), .normalize_repeat(), .normalize_once() implement multi-pass normalization. Global--local index conversion .normalize_once() handles index-space mapping correctly. R/rwisecondorx_cbs.R — .exec_cbs(): CBS wrapper; parallel = TRUE (now default) uses ParDNAcopy::parSegment(num.cores = cpus) explicit thread count; falls back DNAcopy::segment() message ParDNAcopy absent. Matches upstream conventions (0→NA, 0 weights→1e-99, split segments large NA gaps, recalculate weighted means). .get_segment_zscores(): segment-level Z-scores null ratio distributions. R/rwisecondorx_output.R — BED statistics output writers WisecondorXPrediction objects. src/knn_reference.cpp — knn_reference_cpp(): OpenMP-parallelized KNN reference bin finding (leave-one-chromosome-squared Euclidean distance, partial sort K nearest). Stores global 1-based indexes. null_ratios_cpp(): OpenMP-parallelized null ratio computation using global indexes directly full sample vectors. src/nipter_matching.cpp — nipter_ssd_scores_cpp(): one query vs N columns fractions matrix, returns N SSD scores (OpenMP). nipter_ssd_matrix_cpp(): symmetric N×N pairwise SSD matrix (OpenMP, symmetric schedule). accept cpus thread count. src/RcppExports.cpp, R/RcppExports.R — auto-generated Rcpp glue. R/zzz_rcpp.R — @useDynLib RWisecondorX, .registration = TRUE @importFrom Rcpp sourceCpp roxygen directives. src/Makevars, src/Makevars.win — OpenMP compilation flags. Synthetic cohort generator R/synthetic_cohort.R — generate_cohort(): creates synthetic BAMs “compressed” chromosome lengths (100bp per bin) testing. Produces ~435KB BAMs per sample. Trisomy simulation uses Nguyen et al. 2023 fetal fraction model (doi:10.1101/2023.11.24.568620): reads stochastically removed non-target chromosomes probability p = k*f/(1+k*f) simulate relative enrichment trisomy chromosome, k = 0.5 (non-mosaic) k = 0.25 (mosaic). Default fetal fraction 10%. Exports COMPRESSED_BINSIZE constant (100L). inst/scripts/make_cohort.R — CLI wrapper batch cohort generation. Tests — native WisecondorX pipeline inst/tinytest/test_rwisecondorx.R — 76 assertions: reference building (gender model, masking, PCA, KNN indexes distances, null ratios), prediction (normalization, CBS, Z-scores, aberration calling), trisomy detection sensitivity (T21/T18/T13 detected gains, euploid negative control clean). inst/tinytest/test_cohort_pipeline.R — 31 assertions: end--end pipeline (cohort generation → binning → newref → predict → aberration detection). test files use library(RWisecondorX) instead source() hacks. BED.gz reader functions — round-trip stored BED files R/bed_reader.R — bed_to_sample(): reads 4-column BED.gz (bam_convert_bed()) named-list--integer-vectors format rwisecondorx_newref() / rwisecondorx_predict(). bed_to_nipter_sample(): reads 5-column (CombinedStrands) 9-column (SeparatedStrands) BED.gz (nipter_bin_bam_bed()) NIPTeRSample object. Auto-detects column count via read_tabix() probe; handles literal “NA” corrected_count via TRY_CAST. Per-strand corrected values (corrected_fwd, corrected_rev) read independently SeparatedStrands. inst/tinytest/test_bed_reader.R — 46 assertions covering WisecondorX NIPTeR round-trips, SeparatedStrands 9-column BED, corrected per-strand round-trip, sample name inference, scale_sample() integration.

Requires controlled multi-sample BAM set bioconda wisecondorx package via condathis. yet automated. Threading budget propagation: rwisecondorx_newref() accepts cpus KNN finding. rwisecondorx_predict() now accepts cpus passes parSegment(). wired together coherently called pipeline script — use cpus value throughout. Beta-mode aberration calling: rwisecondorx_predict() supports zscore beta (purity-based) cutoffs. beta path (ratio > 2^beta) implemented yet tested real data.

title: RWisecondorX Agent Guidelines
text: “BED.gz” files BGZF-compressed, tabix-indexed TSV files use BED coordinate conventions (0-based half-open intervals, chr prefix). strict BED files — 3 columns extra columns standard BED fields. Always read read_tabix() (DuckDB SQL) Rduckhts::rduckhts_tabix_multi(), never read.table() BED-aware parsers assume 3-column BED semantics. 4-column WisecondorX TSV.bgz: chrom, start, end, count (written bam_convert_bed()). 5-column NIPTeR TSV.bgz (CombinedStrands): chrom, start, end, count, corrected_count (written nipter_bin_bam_bed()). 9-column NIPTeR TSV.bgz (SeparatedStrands): chrom, start, end, count, count_fwd, count_rev, corrected_count, corrected_fwd, corrected_rev (written nipter_bin_bam_bed(separate_strands = TRUE)). count = count_fwd + count_rev; corrected_* columns NA GC-corrected sample supplied. 5-column GC table TSV.bgz: chrom, start, end, pct_gc, seq_len (written nipter_gc_precompute()). Precompute per reference genome + bin size, pass path nipter_gc_correct(gc_table = ...). files bgzipped (BGZF) tabix-indexed via Rduckhts::rduckhts_bgzip() Rduckhts::rduckhts_tabix_index(). use gzfile() external tools. bed_to_sample() reads 4-column TSV.bgz back WisecondorX -memory format. bed_to_nipter_sample() reads 5- 9-column TSV.bgz back NIPTeRSample. nipter_control_group_from_beds() loads whole directory via rduckhts_tabix_multi() one DuckDB pass. reading uses SQL read_tabix() table function — never read_bed(). WisecondorX reference (WisecondorXReference) still RDS-. matrices (PCA, KNN indexes, null ratios) dense numeric arrays sensibly represented flat TSV. Use saveRDS() / readRDS().

title: RWisecondorX Agent Guidelines
text: WisecondorX convert step uses streaming dedup state machine (larp/larp2). exact SQL equivalent uses DuckDB window functions FILE_OFFSET: Key subtleties reproduced exactly: Improper pairs (is_paired is_proper) invisible larp larp2. filter internal rmdup = "streaming" — intrinsic WisecondorX algorithm, user flag option. Unpaired reads update larp larp2 — hence IGNORE NULLS trick. larp never reset chromosomes — hence PARTITION . Bin assignment uses integer division pos // binsize matching Python’s int(pos / binsize). pos 0-based: subtract 1 duckhts 1-based POS dividing.

what: NIPTeR’s scanBam approach is “concat and pretend single-ended”
title: RWisecondorX Agent Guidelines
text: NIPTeR’s bin_bam_sample() calls scanBam() without filtering parameters, splits read records strand (+ / -), discarding reads strand "*" (unmapped). applies unique() positions per chromosome per strand sums forward reverse counts single matrix. consequence paired-end data treated concatenated mates single-ended read pool: R1 (forward) R2 (reverse) counted independently respective start positions. paired-end library produces roughly 2× bin counts single-end library sequencing depth, mates every fragment counted. creates fundamental incompatibility: NIPTeR reference (control group) built single-end samples used test paired-end sample, vice versa. practice goes unnoticed samples cohort use library preparation, mixing single-end paired-end data produces wildly incorrect Z-scores NCV values. nipter_bin_bam() replicates behaviour (mates counted) default (rmdup = "none", flag filters), keeping compatible NIPTeR control groups built way. Users need fragment-level counts (one count per fragment, per read) pre-filter R1 using require_flags = 0x41L (paired + read1) count unique fragments — future enhancement.

what: NIPTeR’s implicit positional dedup is similar to WisecondorX streaming dedup, but different
title: RWisecondorX Agent Guidelines
text: NIPTeR’s bin_reads() applies unique() read positions per chromosome per strand binning. effect two reads start position chromosome strand counted , twice. position-based deduplication. WisecondorX’s larp also collapses consecutive reads position. critical differences: collapse -position reads, NIPTeR’s unique() also silently merges two reads happen share start position, including distinct reads PCR duplicates. hg00106_chr11_fixture.bam fixture (1,018 paired-end reads) NIPTeR’s approach count 1,003 vs 1,018 rmdup = "none" — difference 15 reads, positions duplicate flag (0x400) set one mate.

what: Two additional bugs in NIPTeR’s scanBam call
title: RWisecondorX Agent Guidelines
text: Bug 1 — Split length mismatch BAMs unmapped reads. strands[strands != "*"] length N − U (U = unmapped reads) reads length N. R’s split() requires equal lengths, errors silently misbehaves BAM containing unmapped reads. NIPTeR implicitly requires samtools view -F 4 pre-filtering. Bug 2 — Positional dedup via unique() flag-ignorant. tabulate() counts unique positions per bin, reads. PCR duplicate unique position survives; two distinct reads position silently merged. equivalent -F 1024. Consequence conformance testing. Exact bin--bin match NIPTeR::bin_bam_sample() requires BAM unmapped reads two reads sharing start position per strand. package now bundles fixture inst/extdata/nipter_conformance_fixture.bam; NIPTER_CONFORMANCE_BAM override custom fixture constraints. rmdup = "none" closest mode counts read independently rather unique positions.

what: KNN Index Semantics — Critical Design Note
title: RWisecondorX Agent Guidelines
text: Rcpp implementation (knn_reference_cpp()) stores global 1-based indexes full masked-bin array. Upstream Python (get_ref_for_bins()) stores local indexes relative chr_data (leave-one-chromosome-subset). consequence: global--local conversion .normalize_once() : - Global index g < chr_start → local g (excised chromosome, unchanged) - Global index g > chr_cum → local g - n_chr (excised chromosome, shifted ) - Global index within [chr_start, chr_cum] never occur (-chromosome bins excluded KNN building) change index storage convention without updating .normalize_once() predict null_ratios_cpp() newref.

what: An R Port of WisecondorX for Copy Number Analysis using Duckhts
title: An R Port of WisecondorX for Copy Number Analysis using Duckhts
text: RWisecondorX R toolkit copy number analysis trisomy prediction non-invasive prenatal testing (NIPT), built top Rduckhts DuckDB. ports WisecondorX (CNV detection) NIPTeR (trisomy prediction via Z-scores, NCV, regression, chi-squared correction), following rewrites.bio principles: exact emulation upstream outputs, full credit original authors, transparency AI assistance. core convert step now runs native bam_bin_counts(...) kernel bundled Rduckhts, Python dependency. bam_convert() returns per-bin read counts memory; bam_convert_bed() writes bgzipped, tabix-indexed BED file readable DuckDB tabix-aware tool; bam_convert_npz() serialises WisecondorX-compatible .npz via reticulate. convert implementation exactly replicates upstream larp / larp2 streaming deduplication behaviour, achieving bin--bin agreement Python implementation. NIPTeR statistical layer provides GC correction (via --fly FASTA computation, bundled tables), chi-squared overdispersion correction, chromosomal fraction Z-scores, normalised chromosome values (NCV), forward stepwise regression — operating NIPTeRSample objects produced nipter_bin_bam(). pipelines need full upstream toolchain, thin condathis wrappers cover every stage: wisecondorx_convert(), wisecondorx_newref(), wisecondorx_predict() delegate official bioconda package without requiring manual conda setup.

title: An R Port of WisecondorX for Copy Number Analysis using Duckhts
text: Install development version GitHub: RWisecondorX imports Rduckhts, DBI, duckdb. reticulate needed write .npz files; condathis needed upstream CLI wrappers. Neither required core convert step.

what: Convert A BAM To Bin Counts
title: An R Port of WisecondorX for Copy Number Analysis using Duckhts
text: rmdup argument controls duplicate handling. "streaming" exactly replicates upstream WisecondorX larp / larp2 state machine default. "none" skips deduplication entirely, matching wisecondorx convert --normdup NIPT data read depth low. "flag" uses SAM duplicate flag (0x400) BAMs already processed Picard sambamba.

title: An R Port of WisecondorX for Copy Number Analysis using Duckhts
text: bam_convert_bed() language-agnostic output path. produces bgzipped BED file (chrom, start, end, count; 0-based coordinates) tabix index, using rduckhts_bgzip rduckhts_tabix_index Rduckhts — external tools Python required. bed_to_nipter_sample() reads 5-column NIPTeR BED.gz NIPTeRSample object compatible NIPTeR statistical functions. Column count auto-detected (5 = CombinedStrands, 9 = SeparatedStrands).
code: bed_file <- "sample.wisecondorx.bed.gz"  bam_convert_bed(   bam     = "sample.dm.bam",   bed     = bed_file,   binsize = 100000L,   rmdup   = "streaming" )  # Round-trip: reload into the named-list format for rwisecondorx_newref() sample_reloaded <- bed_to_sample(bed_file, binsize = 100000L)

title: An R Port of WisecondorX for Copy Number Analysis using Duckhts
text: rwisecondorx_newref() rwisecondorx_predict() pure R/Rcpp implementations full WisecondorX algorithm — reference building, PCA correction, within-sample normalisation, CBS segmentation, aberration calling — Python dependency. Performance-critical KNN reference-bin finding compiled via Rcpp OpenMP parallelisation. CBS segmentation uses DNAcopy (ParDNAcopy parallel operation) directly.
code: control_bams <- c("ctrl_01.dm.bam", "ctrl_02.dm.bam", "ctrl_03.dm.bam") control_samples <- lapply(   control_bams,   function(bam) bam_convert(bam, binsize = 100000L, mapq = 1L, rmdup = "streaming") ) names(control_samples) <- sub("\\.bam$", "", basename(control_bams))  ref <- rwisecondorx_newref(   samples = control_samples,   binsize = 100000L,   nipt = TRUE,   cpus = 4L )  test_sample <- bam_convert("test.dm.bam", binsize = 100000L, mapq = 1L, rmdup = "streaming") pred <- rwisecondorx_predict(test_sample, ref, zscore = 5, seed = 42L, cpus = 4L) pred$aberrations

title: An R Port of WisecondorX for Copy Number Analysis using Duckhts
text: full upstream WisecondorX pipeline needed, wisecondorx_convert() wraps wisecondorx convert via condathis produces .npz official Python implementation. three wrappers handle conda environment creation automatically first use. CLI wrappers expose upstream arguments documented WisecondorX README delegate official wisecondorx bioconda package. cohort preprocessing reference building, package now keeps native upstream WisecondorX paths explicit: native rwisecondorx BED.gz files optional upstream wisecondorx NPZ files NIPTeR BED.gz files inst/scripts/build_reference.R --mode rwisecondorx builds native RDS reference BED.gz files inst/scripts/build_reference.R --mode wisecondorx builds upstream NPZ reference NPZ files inst/scripts/wisecondorx_conformance.R takes prepared artifacts (bed + npz test cases, plus references), runs predict paths, compares resulting per-bin _bins.bed outputs
code: wisecondorx_convert(   bam     = "sample.bam",   npz     = "sample.npz",   binsize = 100000L,   normdup = FALSE          # TRUE → --normdup (NIPT) ) fixture_npz <- "sample.npz" np <- reticulate::import("numpy", convert = FALSE)  bam_convert_npz(   bam     = "sample.dm.bam",   npz     = fixture_npz,   binsize = 100000L,   rmdup   = "streaming",   np      = np ) # Requires: condathis installed; wisecondorx bioconda package auto-installed  npz_files <- list.files("controls/", "\\.npz$", full.names = TRUE)  wisecondorx_newref(   npz_files   = npz_files,   output      = "reference.npz",   ref_binsize = 100000L,   nipt        = TRUE,   refsize     = 300L,   cpus        = 4L )  wisecondorx_predict(   npz           = "sample.npz",   ref           = "reference.npz",   output_prefix = "results/sample",   bed           = TRUE,   seed          = 1L )

title: An R Port of WisecondorX for Copy Number Analysis using Duckhts
text: rwisecondorx_predict() supports beta parameter purity-based aberration calling, appropriate somatic CNV analysis tumour purity known. beta supplied, ratio cutoffs replace Z-score thresholds: beta tumour purity estimate (0–1; 0 = liberal calling, 1 = conservative; optimally close true purity). mode appropriate NIPT, uses default Z-score calling.
code: gain cutoff: log2((ploidy + beta/2) / ploidy) loss cutoff: log2((ploidy - beta/2) / ploidy) # Somatic CNV: 40% purity tumour sample pred_somatic <- rwisecondorx_predict(   sample    = tumour_sample,   reference = ref,   beta      = 0.4,   # ~tumour purity   seed      = 1L )

title: An R Port of WisecondorX for Copy Number Analysis using Duckhts
text: RWisecondorX ports statistical analysis layer NIPTeR (de Weerd et al.), replacing Rsamtools Rduckhts bundled sysdata.rda tables --fly computation via rduckhts_fasta_nuc(). analysis pipeline follows NIPTeR’s workflow: bin samples, build control group, correct GC bias overdispersion, compute Z-scores NCV values trisomy prediction.

title: An R Port of WisecondorX for Copy Number Analysis using Duckhts
text: nipter_bin_bam() produces NIPTeRSample objects compatible downstream functions. Pre-filtering flags mirror samtools view -f/-F conventions, real-world NIPT pipelines mark duplicates Picard can pass exclude_flags = 1024L directly.

title: An R Port of WisecondorX for Copy Number Analysis using Duckhts
text: control group reference cohort test samples scored. nipter_as_control_group() validates deduplicates input samples. nipter_diagnose_control_group() reports per-chromosome Z-scores Shapiro-Wilk normality p-values across group, useful identifying outlier samples scoring. working large cohort, nipter_match_control_group() selects closest-matching subset given test sample based chromosomal fraction distance (Rcpp+OpenMP accelerated). nipter_match_matrix() computes full N×N pairwise SSD matrix — useful QC outlier detection building control group.
code: cg <- nipter_as_control_group(samples) diag <- nipter_diagnose_control_group(cg) matched_cg <- nipter_match_control_group(test_sample, cg, n = 50L) mm <- nipter_match_matrix(cg, cpus = 4L)

title: An R Port of WisecondorX for Copy Number Analysis using Duckhts
text: nipter_gc_correct() adjusts bin counts GC-content bias. GC percentages computed per bin reference FASTA via rduckhts_fasta_nuc() rather static bundled tables. Two methods available: LOESS regression (default, matching NIPTeR) bin-weight normalisation. cohort workflows, nipter_gc_precompute() pre-computes saves GC fractions TSV.bgz file FASTA scanned .
code: # Pre-compute GC table (one-time per genome / binsize) nipter_gc_precompute(fasta = "hg38.fa", binsize = 50000L,                      out = "gc_table.tsv.bgz")  # Apply GC correction using pre-computed table (fast) cg          <- nipter_gc_correct(cg, gc_table = "gc_table.tsv.bgz") test_sample <- nipter_gc_correct(test_sample, gc_table = "gc_table.tsv.bgz")

title: An R Port of WisecondorX for Copy Number Analysis using Duckhts
text: nipter_chi_correct() identifies overdispersed bins across control group using normalised chi-squared test downweights . correction applied simultaneously test sample control group, maintaining consistency downstream scoring.
code: corrected   <- nipter_chi_correct(test_sample, cg, chi_cutoff = 3.5) test_sample <- corrected$sample cg          <- corrected$control_group

title: An R Port of WisecondorX for Copy Number Analysis using Duckhts
text: nipter_z_score() computes chromosomal fraction Z-score focus chromosome. Z-score 3 conventional threshold trisomy. nipter_ncv_score() computes normalised chromosome value, searching denominator chromosome set minimises control-group variance computing Z-score. robust standard Z-score control group small. nipter_regression() uses forward stepwise linear regression predict focus chromosome’s fraction chromosomes, scores residual.
code: z21 <- nipter_z_score(test_sample, cg, chromo_focus = 21) z21$sample_z_score z21$control_statistics ncv21 <- nipter_ncv_score(test_sample, cg, chromo_focus = 21) ncv21$sample_ncv_score ncv21$denominators reg21 <- nipter_regression(test_sample, cg, chromo_focus = 21) reg21$models[[1]]$z_score reg21$models[[1]]$predictors

title: An R Port of WisecondorX for Copy Number Analysis using Duckhts
text: nipter_sex_model() trains 2-component Gaussian mixture model chromosome fraction data control group sex prediction. Three model types available: Y-fraction alone, bivariate X+Y fractions, Y-unique region read ratios specific Y-chromosome genes. nipter_predict_sex() classifies test sample using one models majority-vote consensus (ties → female, conservative choice NIPT).
code: # Requires: mclust package; whole-genome BAMs; FASTA for GC correction cg <- nipter_as_control_group(samples) cg <- nipter_gc_correct(cg, fasta = "hg38.fa") corrected <- nipter_chi_correct(samples[[1]], cg) cg <- corrected$control_group  model_y  <- nipter_sex_model(cg, method = "y_fraction") model_xy <- nipter_sex_model(cg, method = "xy_fraction")  nipter_predict_sex(test_sample, models = list(model_y, model_xy))

code: bam_convert(   bam,   binsize = 5000L,   mapq = 1L,   require_flags = 0L,   exclude_flags = 0L,   rmdup = c("streaming", "flag", "none"),   separate_strands = FALSE,   con = NULL,   reference = NULL )

title: Count reads per bin from a BAM or CRAM file — bam_convert
text: bam Path indexed BAM CRAM file. binsize Bin size base pairs. Default 5000L (WisecondorX); use 50000L NIPTeR-style workflows. mapq Minimum mapping quality. Default 1L (WisecondorX / samtools default). Set 0L retain reads regardless MAPQ (NIPTeR). require_flags Integer bitmask. reads (FLAG & require_flags) == require_flags retained. 0L (default) imposes requirement. Example: require_flags = 0x2L keeps properly paired reads. exclude_flags Integer bitmask. Reads (FLAG & exclude_flags) != 0 dropped. 0L (default) drops nothing. Example: exclude_flags = 0xF04L drops unmapped, secondary, QC-fail supplementary reads (common samtools pre-filter). rmdup Duplicate removal strategy. "streaming" (default) applies WisecondorX larp/larp2 algorithm (also excludes improper pairs). "flag" drops reads SAM flag 0x400. "none" keeps reads pass filters. separate_strands Logical; TRUE, returns per-strand counts (forward + reverse -). return value changes list two named lists (fwd rev), structured like default return. Used nipter_bin_bam(separate_strands = TRUE) NIPTeR SeparatedStrands object. Default FALSE. con Optional open DBI connection duckhts already loaded. NULL (default) temporary -memory DuckDB connection created. reference Optional FASTA reference path CRAM inputs.

title: Count reads per bin from a BAM or CRAM file — bam_convert
text: separate_strands = FALSE (default): list-like WisecondorXSample S7 object one integer vector per chromosome key ("1"–"22", "23" X, "24" Y). vector contains per-bin read counts (bin 0 = positions 0 binsize - 1). Chromosomes present BAM header returned dense vectors padded trailing zeros chromosome span implied header binsize; chromosomes absent header NULL. separate_strands = TRUE: list two elements, fwd rev, structured like default return.

title: Count reads per bin from a BAM or CRAM file — bam_convert
text: Read filtering mirrors samtools view convention: mapq sets minimum mapping quality; require_flags bitmask flags must set (equivalent samtools view -f); exclude_flags bitmask flags must clear (equivalent samtools view -F).  Use duckhts UDF sam_flag_bits(flag) inspect named flag fields, sam_flag_has(flag, bit) test individual bits. rmdup controls duplicate removal independently flag filters: "streaming" applies WisecondorX larp/larp2 consecutive-position state machine also enforces WisecondorX improper-pair rule (paired reads properly paired excluded counting dedup state — intrinsic algorithm, flag option); "flag" additionally excludes reads SAM flag 0x400 set (Picard / sambamba pre-marked duplicates); "none" applies deduplication. heavy lifting delegated native bam_bin_counts(...) kernel bundled Rduckhts; RWisecondorX reshapes fixed-bin output chromosome-keyed objects expected WisecondorX NIPTeR layers.

what: Convert BAM/CRAM to a bgzipped BED bin-count file — bam_convert_bed
title: Convert BAM/CRAM to a bgzipped BED bin-count file — bam_convert_bed
text: Runs bam_convert() writes per-bin read counts bgzipped, tab-delimited BED file (four columns: chrom, start, end, count), creates tabix index (.tbi) alongside via Rduckhts. language-agnostic intermediate format RWisecondorX native pipeline; file can queried directly duckhts/DuckDB tabix-aware tool.

title: Convert BAM/CRAM to a bgzipped BED bin-count file — bam_convert_bed
code: bam_convert_bed(   bam,   bed,   binsize = 5000L,   mapq = 1L,   require_flags = 0L,   exclude_flags = 0L,   rmdup = c("streaming", "flag", "none"),   con = NULL,   reference = NULL,   index = TRUE )

title: Convert BAM/CRAM to a bgzipped BED bin-count file — bam_convert_bed
text: bam Path indexed BAM CRAM file. bed Path output .bed.gz file (created overwritten). tabix index written paste0(bed, ".tbi"). binsize Bin size base pairs (default 5000). mapq Minimum mapping quality (default 1L). require_flags Integer bitmask; reads bits set kept (samtools -f). Default 0L (requirement). exclude_flags Integer bitmask; reads bit set dropped (samtools -F). Default 0L (nothing dropped). rmdup Duplicate-removal strategy passed bam_convert(). con Optional open DBI connection duckhts already loaded. NULL (default), temporary -memory DuckDB connection created. reference Optional FASTA reference path CRAM inputs. index Logical; TRUE (default) tabix index created alongside bgzipped output.

title: Convert BAM/CRAM to a bgzipped BED bin-count file — bam_convert_bed
text: bed (invisibly).

title: Convert BAM/CRAM to a bgzipped BED bin-count file — bam_convert_bed
text: Coordinates 0-based half-open intervals matching BED convention (start = bin_index * binsize, end = start + binsize). Chromosomes written 1–22, X, Y (chr prefix) numeric order. header-defined bins written, including count zero. bgzip tabix indexing performed via Rduckhts::rduckhts_bgzip() Rduckhts::rduckhts_tabix_index(), external tools required.

title: Convert BAM/CRAM to WisecondorX NPZ format — bam_convert_npz
text: bam Path indexed BAM CRAM file. npz Path output .npz file (created overwritten). binsize Bin size base pairs (default 5000). mapq Minimum mapping quality passed bam_convert(). require_flags Integer bitmask required SAM flags passed bam_convert(). exclude_flags Integer bitmask excluded SAM flags passed bam_convert(). rmdup Duplicate-removal strategy passed bam_convert(). con Optional open DBI connection duckhts already loaded. Passed bam_convert(). np Optional numpy module imported via reticulate::import("numpy"). NULL (default) imported automatically. reference Optional FASTA reference path CRAM inputs. Passed bam_convert().

title: Convert BAM/CRAM to WisecondorX NPZ format — bam_convert_npz
text: npz (invisibly).

title: Convert BAM/CRAM to WisecondorX NPZ format — bam_convert_npz
text: resulting NPZ three top-level keys: sample 0-d object array wrapping dict mapping "1".."24" int32 arrays (chromosome bin counts). binsize Scalar int (bin size bp). quality 0-d object array wrapping dict QC counters (populated zeros since native binning kernel track per-read filter stats). writer's numpy >= 2.0 internal pickle references numpy._core, older numpy (< 2.0) unpickle.  function patches pickle bytestream use numpy.core NPZ readable numpy 1.x 2.x. Native bam_convert() returns dense vectors covering chromosome span BAM header. Upstream wisecondorx convert one additional historical quirk: allocates int(length / binsize + 1) bins every chromosome header, chromosomes whose lengths exact multiples binsize carry one extra trailing -zero bin. function pads upstream NPZ layout serialisation Python wisecondorx newref/predict remains byte-compatible, native R list/BED paths keep cleaner header-span contract.

NULL (default), derived BED file basename (e.g. "sample" "sample.bed.gz"). binsize Optional integer; bin size base pairs. NULL (default), inferred first row BED file. con Optional open DBI connection duckhts already loaded.

title: Read a NIPTeR-format BED file into a NIPTeRSample — bed_to_nipter_sample
text: object class c("NIPTeRSample", <strand_type>): CombinedStrands (5-column BED): structure nipter_bin_bam() separate_strands = FALSE. SeparatedStrands (9-column BED): structure nipter_bin_bam() separate_strands = TRUE.

title: Read a NIPTeR-format BED file into a NIPTeRSample — bed_to_nipter_sample
text: 5-column BED (chrom, start, end, count, corrected_count) produces CombinedStrands sample. 9-column BED (chrom, start, end, count, count_fwd, count_rev, corrected_count, corrected_fwd, corrected_rev) produces SeparatedStrands sample independent forward/reverse count matrices. number columns detected automatically. corrected columns contain non-NA values (.e. BED written GC-corrected corrected argument), returned sample's count matrices replaced corrected values correction status set "GC Corrected". SeparatedStrands, per-strand corrected values (corrected_fwd, corrected_rev) used populate forward reverse matrices independently.

title: Read a NIPTeR-format BED file into a NIPTeRSample — bed_to_nipter_sample
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Write NIPTeR-style bin counts to BED, then read back nipter_bin_bam_bed("sample.bam", "sample.bed.gz") sample <- bed_to_nipter_sample("sample.bed.gz")  # Use with NIPTeR scoring samples <- lapply(bed_files, bed_to_nipter_sample) cg <- nipter_as_control_group(samples) z21 <- nipter_z_score(samples[[1]], cg, chromo_focus = 21) } # }

what: Bin a BAM/CRAM file — NIPTeR style — nipter_bin_bam
title: Bin a BAM/CRAM file — NIPTeR style — nipter_bin_bam
text: Replicates NIPTeR's bin_bam_sample(): counts reads fixed-width bins across chromosomes 1-22, X, Y. original NIPTeR counts mapped reads MAPQ filter; real-world NIPT pipelines typically pre-filter samtools view --min-MQ 40 -F 1024 binning. modes supported mapq, exclude_flags, require_flags parameters.

title: Bin a BAM/CRAM file — NIPTeR style — nipter_bin_bam
code: nipter_bin_bam(   bam,   binsize = 50000L,   mapq = 0L,   require_flags = 0L,   exclude_flags = 0L,   rmdup = c("none", "flag"),   separate_strands = FALSE,   con = NULL,   reference = NULL )

title: Bin a BAM/CRAM file — NIPTeR style — nipter_bin_bam
text: bam Path indexed BAM CRAM file. binsize Bin size base pairs. Default 50000 (NIPTeR's fixed bin size). Must match binsize used building control group. mapq Minimum mapping quality. Default 0L (NIPTeR original: mapped reads). Set 40L match common NIPT pipeline pre-filtering (samtools view --min-MQ 40). require_flags Integer bitmask; reads bits set kept (samtools -f). Default 0L (requirement). exclude_flags Integer bitmask; reads bit set dropped (samtools -F). Default 0L. Set 1024L (0x400) exclude reads marked duplicates (samtools view -F 1024). rmdup Duplicate removal strategy. "none" (default, NIPTeR standard) "flag" (equivalent exclude_flags = 1024L; BAMs already processed Picard / sambamba). NIPTeR perform streaming deduplication. separate_strands Logical; TRUE, produces SeparatedStrands object independent forward/reverse count matrices. Default FALSE (CombinedStrands). con Optional open DBI connection duckhts already loaded. reference Optional FASTA reference path CRAM inputs.

title: Bin a BAM/CRAM file — NIPTeR style — nipter_bin_bam
text: object class c("NIPTeRSample", <strand_type>): CombinedStrands (default): autosomal_chromosome_reads list one 22-row integer matrix (rows "1"–"22"); sex_chromosome_reads list one 2-row matrix (rows "X", "Y"). SeparatedStrands (separate_strands = TRUE): autosomal_chromosome_reads list two matrices — element 1 forward (rows "1F"–"22F"), element 2 reverse (rows "1R"–"22R"); sex_chromosome_reads similarly contains forward ("XF", "YF") reverse ("XR", "YR") matrices.

title: Bin a BAM/CRAM file — NIPTeR style — nipter_bin_bam
text: result reshaped NIPTeRSample object whose structure parallels NIPTeR's NIPTSample: autosomal reads chromosome--bin matrix sex chromosome reads separate two-row matrix. separate_strands = TRUE, forward (+) reverse (-) reads counted independently, producing two matrices per chromosome set (class "SeparatedStrands"). doubles predictor pool nipter_regression() — see NIPTeR documentation details.

title: Bin a BAM/CRAM file — NIPTeR style — nipter_bin_bam
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # NIPTeR original defaults: all mapped reads, no dedup sample <- nipter_bin_bam("sample.bam", binsize = 50000L)  # Common NIPT pipeline: MAPQ >= 40, exclude duplicate-flagged reads sample <- nipter_bin_bam("sample.dm.bam", binsize = 50000L,                          mapq = 40L, exclude_flags = 1024L)  # SeparatedStrands for regression with doubled predictor pool sample_ss <- nipter_bin_bam("sample.bam", separate_strands = TRUE)  sample$autosomal_chromosome_reads[[1]]["21", ]   # chr21 bin counts } # }

what: Chi-squared correction for overdispersed bins — nipter_chi_correct
title: Chi-squared correction for overdispersed bins — nipter_chi_correct
text: Identifies bins control group excess variance (overdispersion) using chi-squared test downweights . reduces influence noisy bins downstream Z-scores NCV calculations.

title: Chi-squared correction for overdispersed bins — nipter_chi_correct
code: nipter_chi_correct(   sample,   control_group,   chi_cutoff = 3.5,   include_sex = FALSE )

title: Chi-squared correction for overdispersed bins — nipter_chi_correct
text: sample NIPTeRSample object (test sample). control_group NIPTeRControlGroup object. chi_cutoff Normalised chi-squared threshold. Bins (chi - df) / sqrt(2*df) > chi_cutoff corrected. Default 3.5. include_sex Logical; correct sex chromosomes well? Default FALSE. Sex chromosome bins use chi-squared weights derived autosomes.

title: Chi-squared correction for overdispersed bins — nipter_chi_correct
text: list two elements: sample corrected NIPTeRSample. control_group corrected NIPTeRControlGroup.

title: Chi-squared correction for overdispersed bins — nipter_chi_correct
text: correction applied simultaneously test sample control group samples, maintaining consistency. algorithm follows NIPTeR's chi-squared correction: control sample, scale autosomal bin counts total reads match overall mean across control samples. Compute expected count per bin (mean scaled counts). Compute chi-squared per bin: \(\chi^2 = \sum_i (expected - scaled_i)^2 / expected\). Normalise: \(z = (\chi^2 - df) / \sqrt{2 \cdot df}\) \(df = n_{controls} - 1\). bins \(z > \text{chi\_cutoff}\): divide reads \(\chi^2 / df\).

title: Chi-squared correction for overdispersed bins — nipter_chi_correct

title: GC-correct a NIPTeR sample or control group — nipter_gc_correct
text: object NIPTeRSample NIPTeRControlGroup. fasta Path indexed reference FASTA file (.fa/.fasta .fai index). Ignored gc_table supplied. method GC correction method: "loess" (default) "bin" (bin-weight). span LOESS smoothing parameter (used method = "loess"). Default 0.75. include_sex Logical; correct sex chromosomes (X, Y) well? Default FALSE. binsize Bin size used binning sample (default 50000L). Must match binsize sample. Ignored gc_table list (bin size already encoded vector lengths). gc_table Pre-computed GC table. Either path TSV.bgz file (nipter_gc_precompute) -memory named list returned previous .get_gc_table() call. NULL (default), GC content computed fasta. con Optional open DBI connection duckhts loaded. NULL, temporary connection created.

title: GC-correct a NIPTeR sample or control group — nipter_gc_correct
text: corrected copy object class. Correction status updated "Uncorrected" "GC Corrected".

title: GC-correct a NIPTeR sample or control group — nipter_gc_correct
text: GC content can supplied three ways via gc_table parameter: Pre-computed file Path TSV.bgz produced nipter_gc_precompute. Fastest large cohorts — compute , reuse every sample. -memory list Named list numeric vectors (one per chromosome) returned .get_gc_table(). Useful chaining corrections within session. FASTA path via fasta Compute GC --fly every call. Convenient single-sample use; slow many samples. LOESS method (default): Fits LOESS curve read counts vs GC percentage across autosomal bins known GC non-zero reads. bin scaled median(counts) / fitted(loess), bins normalised genome-wide median. NIPTeR default method. Bin-weight method: Groups bins GC percentage (0.1\ computes mean read count per GC bucket, scales bin global_mean / bucket_mean. Faster LOESS less smooth. Sex chromosome correction (include_sex = TRUE) uses nearest-neighbour lookup autosomal LOESS curve (LOESS method) GC bucket weights (bin-weight method).

text: sample NIPTeRSample object. control_group NIPTeRControlGroup object. chromo_focus Integer; target chromosome (1-22). n_models Number regression models build (default 4). n_predictors Maximum number predictor chromosomes per model (default 4). exclude_chromosomes Integer vector chromosomes exclude predictor pool (default c(13, 18, 21)). include_chromosomes Integer vector chromosomes force-include. train_fraction Fraction control samples used training (default 0.6). overdispersion_rate Overdispersion multiplier theoretical CV (default 1.15). force_practical_cv Logical; always use practical CV regardless theoretical CV? Default FALSE. seed Random seed train/test split. Default NULL (seed set).

title: Regression-based Z-score for trisomy prediction — nipter_regression
text: list class "NIPTeRRegression" elements: models list n_models sublists, containing: z_score (numeric), cv (selected CV), cv_type ("practical" "theoretical"), predictors (character vector predictor chromosomes), shapiro_p_value, control_z_scores (named numeric vector). focus_chromosome Character. correction_status Character vector. sample_name Character.

title: Regression-based Z-score for trisomy prediction — nipter_regression
text: SeparatedStrands samples predictor pool doubled (autosomal chromosome contributes forward reverse fraction), complementary-strand exclusion within model prevents selecting strands chromosome predictors model. Across models, exact predictor string excluded; complementary strand remains available. algorithm: Split control group 60/40 train test sets. Compute chromosomal fractions samples. n_models models, greedily select n_predictors chromosomes maximising adjusted R-squared (forward stepwise selection). Predictors already used previous models excluded. model, fit lm(focus ~ predictors) training set, predict test set, compute: Practical CV: sd(obs/pred) / mean(obs/pred) test set. Theoretical CV: overdispersion_rate / sqrt(total_reads_focus) test sample. larger CV used (unless force_practical_cv = TRUE). Sample Z-score: (sample_ratio - 1) / cv.

text: control_group NIPTeRControlGroup object. Ideally chi-squared corrected via nipter_chi_correct. method Character; feature space GMM: "y_fraction" Univariate: Y-chromosome read fraction relative total autosomal reads. "xy_fraction" Bivariate: X Y chromosome fractions.

title: Build a sex prediction model from a NIPTeR control group — nipter_sex_model
text: list-like NIPTeRSexModel S7 object elements: model mclust::Mclust fitted object. method Character; method used. male_cluster Integer (1 2); cluster male (higher Y fraction). classifications Named character vector "male"/ "female" labels control sample. fractions Matrix input features used fitting (samples rows). Columns "y_fraction" c("x_fraction", "y_fraction").

title: Build a sex prediction model from a NIPTeR control group — nipter_sex_model
text: mirrors approach used clinical NIPT pipelines (see Details), ported R using mclust::Mclust() place Python's sklearn.GaussianMixture. algorithm: Compute sex chromosome read fractions every sample control group. Y fraction = sum(Y bins) / sum(autosomal bins); X fraction = sum(X bins) / sum(autosomal bins). Fit two-component Gaussian mixture equal mixing proportions (mclust::Mclust(data, G = 2,     control = mclust::emControl(equalPro = TRUE))). Identify male cluster component higher median Y fraction. follows clinical NIPT pipeline pattern building sex models control cohorts. user's pipeline builds three models (Y-unique ratio samtools, XY fractions, Y fraction) takes majority vote; nipter_predict_sex() implements consensus given multiple models.

title: Build a sex prediction model from a NIPTeR control group — nipter_sex_model

title: Build a WisecondorX reference from binned samples — rwisecondorx_newref
text: pipeline trains gender model (2-component GMM Y-fractions), optionally applies gender correction non-NIPT workflows, computes global bin mask, builds three sub-references: autosomes (), female gonosomes (F), male gonosomes (M). sub-reference includes PCA components, within-sample reference bin indices distances, null ratios -sample Z-scoring. faithful port upstream Python wisecondorx newref, crediting original WisecondorX authors. returned reference object contains: binsize Integer; reference bin size. is_nipt Logical; whether NIPT mode used. trained_cutoff Numeric; Y-fraction gender cutoff. has_female Logical; whether female gonosomal reference exists. has_male Logical; whether male gonosomal reference exists. mask, bins_per_chr, masked_bins_per_chr, masked_bins_per_chr_cum, pca_components, pca_mean, indexes, distances, null_ratios Autosomal reference components. mask.F, ..., null_ratios.F Female gonosomal reference (present). mask.M, ..., null_ratios.M Male gonosomal reference (present).

title: RWisecondorX 0.0.0.9001 (development version)
text: package script-level reference-building CLIs now aligned strictly. build_reference.R now defaults WisecondorX references 100kb target binsize, keeps NIPTeR 50kb binsize path, errors NIPTeR BED-mode flags (--ref-binsize, --gc-table, --fasta, --separate-strands) previously accepted ignored. New nipter_control_group_qc() returns typed NIPTControlGroupQC object control-cohort diagnostics. exposes per-chromosome mean/SD/CV/Shapiro summaries, per-sample matching/outlier summaries, sex-stratified X/Y readiness metrics, optional bin-level chi-profile summaries including expected counts, scaled mean/SD/CV, chi scores, correction factors. chi-correction implementation now reuses explicit internal chi-profile builder instead recomputing discarding bin-level statistics. keeps correction path new QC/report path calculation. Gaunosome model building scoring now fail explicitly invalid reference subsets missing inputs. Sex-specific NCV/regression builders error filtered female male subset small, NCV scoring now checks requested numerator denominator chromosome columns actually present, regression sex scores centred learned mean_ratio, tied sex-model votes now warn instead silently picking class. NIPTeR S7 layer tightened around control-group abstraction. NIPTControlGroup objects now carry optional explicit sample_sex annotations plus sex_source, public nipter_as_control_group() / nipter_control_group_from_beds() builders accept labels directly. New nipter_reference_frame() builds chromosome-level training table (NChrReads_* / FrChrReads_*) directly control group, optional SampleSex column. package-level foundation future gaunosome model building instead pushing production-specific data munging core sample classes. NIPTeR modelling layer now typed S7 reference/model objects: NIPTReferenceFrame, NIPTeRSexModel, NIPTeRSexPrediction, NIPTReferenceModel. New nipter_build_reference() packages control group, reference frame, built sex models one validated reference object, nipter_predict_sex() now accepts reference object directly. nipter_build_reference() now enriches embedded NIPTReferenceFrame production-style sex-scoring columns (ConsensusGender, RR_X, RR_Y, sex-class leave-one-z-scores, IsRefSexOutlier) instead leaving gaunosome bookkeeping downstream application code. New NIPTSexScore / nipter_sex_score() score X/Y directly sex-matched non-outlier reference subset owned typed reference object. New nipter_build_sex_ncv_models() / nipter_build_sex_regression_models() attach typed gaunosome NCV regression models directly NIPTReferenceModel, nipter_ncv_sex_score() / nipter_regression_sex_score() now score X/Y package-owned reference state instead requiring loose production-script data frames model lists. New nipter_build_gaunosome_models() nipter_gaunosome_score() provide package-level gaunosome API top NIPTReferenceModel: one precomputation step X/Y NCV regression models, one typed aggregate score object bundling sex z-scores, NCV scores, regression scores, compact per-chromosome summary table. New nipter_gaunosome_report() write_nipter_gaunosome_output() provide cohort/reporting layer top typed gaunosome API: batch sample scoring now returns one validated report object flattened summary table, summary can written directly tab-separated output file without rebuilding downstream script logic. S7 implementation now exposes compatibility accessors legacy autosomal_chromosome_reads, sex_chromosome_reads, sample_name, correction-status fields, control-group fraction cache now real environment-backed cache instead dead placeholder. NIPTeR S7 compatibility layer longer registers bare $ methods upstream NIPTeR class names. fixes recursive dispatch bug overflow C stack cross-package conformance tests, keeping compatibility RWisecondorX’s namespaced S7 objects. Raw NIPTeR bin-count samples now preserve integer storage uncorrected autosomal sex matrices. corrected matrices promoted double, restores original count semantics round-trip binning tests. WisecondorX objects now use real S7 hierarchy: WisecondorXSample, WisecondorXReference, WisecondorXPrediction, WisecondorXReferenceQC validated S7 classes intentionally subclass list, existing list-style code keeps working object boundary now typed explicit. New simulate_trisomy_bam() creates BAM-level trisomy simulation donor BAM/CRAM thinning non-target chromosomes htslib-backed native implementation. preserves donor read layout sort order, writes real BAM, indexes Rduckhts. New inst/scripts/simulate_trisomy_bam.R exposes primitive CLI. New simulate_trisomy_cohort() batches donor-BAM simulator across donor set simulation grid, writes cohort manifest, reuses one Rduckhts indexing session whole run. New inst/scripts/simulate_trisomy_cohort.R exposes cohort generator  New rwisecondorx_ref_qc() mirrors upstream Python ref_qc.py heuristics native WisecondorXReference objects. returns structured PASS/WARN/FAIL report R can optionally write report JSON later inspection, without requiring Python wisecondorx package. wisecondorx_newref() now defaults cpus = 4L, matching native rwisecondorx_newref() implementation package CLI defaults. removes unnecessary single-thread slow path public Python wrapper. wisecondorx_*() CLI wrappers now isolate condathis/libmamba cache state shared ~/.cache/mamba tree setting session-local XDG_CACHE_HOME environment creation command execution. avoids stale proc.lock failures unrelated mamba sessions. Python wisecondorx_*() wrappers now cache successful condathis environment setup rest R session force condathis run known-existing working directory. avoids redundant create_env() calls fixes libmamba get current path failure prior subprocess test left R invalid deleted current directory. Python conformance tests longer convert live wisecondorx runtime failures skips. Missing optional dependencies (condathis, reticulate, numpy) still skip, environment creation, upstream wisecondorx convert/newref/predict, Python-side NPZ loading now fail loudly contextual errors. bam_convert() now keeps native dense-bin contract Rduckhts: chromosomes present BAM header come back trailing-zero-padded vectors across full header span, bam_convert_npz() adds extra upstream WisecondorX int(length / binsize + 1) NPZ padding quirk serialisation time. NPZ tests now assert exact padded lengths instead comparing shared prefixes. bundled nipter_conformance_fixture.bam now treated real package invariant rather optional convenience. generator validates every chromosome 1-22/X/Y forward reverse reads, fixture tests assert property. Live cross-package checks installed NIPTeR package now skip explicit message upstream NIPTeR::bin_bam_sample() incompatible current Rsamtools/Bioconductor stack; always-inline formula fixture tests remain strict.

New tests corrected per-strand round-trip: CombinedStrands corrected values SeparatedStrands per-strand corrected values (independent forward/reverse multipliers) survive write→read cycle within floating-point tolerance. Total test count: 391 assertions (46 test_bed_reader.R), passing.

title: RWisecondorX 0.0.0.9001 (development version)
text: New rwisecondorx_newref() — pure-R/Rcpp implementation WisecondorX newref pipeline. Takes list binned samples (bam_convert()) builds PCA-based reference: gender model training (2-component GMM Y-fractions), global bin masking, per-partition normalize/PCA/filter/KNN reference building, null ratio computation. Supports NIPT mode, custom Y-fraction cutoff, multi-threaded KNN via OpenMP. New rwisecondorx_predict() — pure-R implementation WisecondorX predict pipeline. Coverage normalization, PCA projection, iterative within-sample normalization aberration masking, gonosomal normalization, result inflation, log-transformation, optional blacklist masking, CBS segmentation (via DNAcopy ParDNAcopy), segment Z-scoring, aberration calling. Supports Z-score beta/ratio calling modes. New rwisecondorx_write_bins_bed(), rwisecondorx_write_segments_bed(), rwisecondorx_write_aberrations_bed(), rwisecondorx_write_statistics() writing prediction results BED statistics files. New scale_sample() rescaling binned samples different bin sizes (e.g. 5kb → 100kb). R/rwisecondorx_utils.R — shared utilities including .train_gender_model() (mclust NULL fallback zero-variance Y-fractions -female cohorts), .gender_correct(), .get_mask(), .normalize_and_mask(), .train_pca(), .project_pc(), .predict_gender(). R/rwisecondorx_cbs.R — .exec_cbs() wrapper around DNAcopy’s segment() upstream WisecondorX conventions (0→NA, 0 weights→1e-99, split segments large NA gaps, recalculate weighted means). Supports ParDNAcopy parallel segmentation.

what: Rcpp acceleration for KNN reference building
title: RWisecondorX 0.0.0.9001 (development version)
text: New src/knn_reference.cpp implementing knn_reference_cpp() null_ratios_cpp() C++ OpenMP parallelization. Replaces O(n_bins² × n_samples) pure-R double -loop compiled code. KNN reference-finding step previously took minutes now completes seconds. Rcpp added Imports LinkingTo DESCRIPTION. OpenMP flags src/Makevars src/Makevars.win.

Global index g now correctly mapped local space: g excluded chromosome, g - n_chr . bug caused reference bins looked wrong positions within-sample normalization. what: Fix: mclust GMM on zero-variance Y-fractions
title: RWisecondorX 0.0.0.9001 (development version)
text: .train_gender_model() now handles case female samples exactly 0.0 Y-fraction (Y reads). mclust::Mclust(..., G=2) returns NULL scenario; fix falls back gap-based cutoff: min(nonzero_y_fractions) / 2.

title: RWisecondorX 0.0.0.9001 (development version)
text: New generate_cohort() creates synthetic BAM files testing using “compressed” chromosome lengths (100bp per bin instead 100kb). Produces identical bin COUNT structure GRCh37 100kb resolution ~435KB BAMs. Supports injecting trisomy signal (extra reads target chromosome). New COMPRESSED_BINSIZE constant (100L) exported use bam_convert(binsize = COMPRESSED_BINSIZE). inst/scripts/make_cohort.R — CLI wrapper cohort generation.

title: RWisecondorX 0.0.0.9001 (development version)
text: test files now use library(RWisecondorX) instead source() hacks load R code directly. required functions call compiled C++ code (knn_reference_cpp, null_ratios_cpp) available installed package. Fixed tinytest assertion: expect_message(..., pattern = NA) mean “expect messages” — fails message emitted. Replaced expect_silent(). New inst/tinytest/test_rwisecondorx.R — 76 assertions covering reference building (gender model, masking, PCA, KNN indexes, null ratios), prediction (normalization, CBS, aberration calling), trisomy detection sensitivity. New inst/tinytest/test_cohort_pipeline.R — 31 assertions end--end pipeline: cohort generation → binning → reference building → prediction → trisomy detection. Tests trisomy 21, 18, 13 detected gains euploid samples produce aberrations. Total test count: 345 assertions, passing.

what: Y-unique region ratio for sex prediction
title: RWisecondorX 0.0.0.9001 (development version)
text: New nipter_y_unique_ratio() counts reads overlapping 7 Y-chromosome unique gene regions (HSFY1, BPY2, BPY2B, BPY2C, XKRY, PRY, PRY2) computes ratio total nuclear genome reads. Uses DuckDB/duckhts index-based region queries (read_bam(region := ...)) efficient BAM access. bundled GRCh37 regions file can replaced custom file assemblies. New nipter_sex_model_y_unique() fits 2-component GMM Y-unique ratios (one per BAM cohort), producing NIPTeRSexModel compatible nipter_predict_sex(). nipter_predict_sex() gains y_unique_ratio parameter passing pre-computed Y-unique ratio "y_unique" model included consensus vote. enables full 3-model majority-vote sex prediction pipeline (Y-unique ratio + Y fraction + XY fractions). Bundled inst/extdata/grch37_Y_UniqueRegions.txt — TSV 7 GRCh37 Y-unique regions used Y-unique ratio calculation.

what: Sex prediction via Gaussian mixture models
title: RWisecondorX 0.0.0.9001 (development version)
text: New nipter_sex_model() fits 2-component GMM sex chromosome fractions NIPTeRControlGroup using mclust::Mclust(). Supports "y_fraction" (univariate Y-chromosome fraction) "xy_fraction" (bivariate X + Y fractions) methods. male cluster identified component higher median Y fraction. New nipter_predict_sex() classifies NIPTeRSample male female given one NIPTeRSexModel objects. Multiple models use majority vote consensus (tie defaults “female” — conservative NIPT). mclust added Suggests DESCRIPTION.

title: RWisecondorX 0.0.0.9001 (development version)
text: nipter_bin_bam_bed() gains separate_strands parameter. TRUE, outputs 9-column BED (chrom, start, end, count, count_fwd, count_rev, corrected_count, corrected_fwd, corrected_rev) count = count_fwd + count_rev. FALSE (default), 5-column BED format unchanged.

title: RWisecondorX 0.0.0.9001 (development version)
text: bam_convert() gains separate_strands parameter. TRUE, returns list fwd (forward strand) rev (reverse strand) data frames. nipter_bin_bam() gains separate_strands = TRUE support, producing NIPTeRSample objects class c("NIPTeRSample", "SeparatedStrands"). Autosomal chromosome reads stored list two matrices (forward reverse) rownames "1F".."22F" "1R".."22R". NIPTeR statistical functions now support SeparatedStrands samples: nipter_gc_correct(): LOESS/bin-weight fitted summed strand counts, corrections applied independently strand matrix. nipter_chi_correct(): chi-squared computed summed strands, correction applied per-strand. nipter_z_score() nipter_ncv_score(): use collapsed (F+R) fractions. nipter_regression(): doubles predictor pool (44 candidates: "1F".."22F", "1R".."22R") complementary exclusion within model (selecting "5F" excludes "5F" "5R").

title: RWisecondorX 0.0.0.9001 (development version)
